title: Binary counter increment adds exactly one
statement_informal: Incrementing a little-endian binary counter adds one. inc ripples the carry: a 0 bit flips to 1 and stops; a 1 bit flips to 0 and carries on. Reading the bits back (Horner base 2): from_bits (inc l) = S (from_bits l). Correctness companion to binary-counter-increments (its amortized O(1) cost).

cost_model.notes: inc flips bits until the first 0 (ripple carry). Correctness from_bits (inc l) = S (from_bits l) by induction (case on the low bit), closed by lia.

references: Binary counter increment (TAOCP Vol. 4A). Correctness (value + 1); amortized cost is work-units/binary-counter-increments.
